description: Manual brief from dashboard for the first Meadow Lane Dental long-form piece. Topic on how to choose a private dentist — awareness-stage content for middle-aged Didsbury residents considering going private. Agent should produce a ~1,500-word piece citing 4-6 authoritative sources.
brief_inline:
# First Content Brief — Meadow Lane Dental

## Deliverable
Long-form article: "What to look for in a private dentist in 2026 — an honest guide"

## Target audience
Middle-aged (35–60) Didsbury and South Manchester residents considering moving from NHS to private dental care. Value quality and long-term relationship over lowest price. Likely to have researched 2-3 practices already.

## Objective
Consideration stage. Filter for the ideal Meadow Lane patient — someone who values transparency, continuity of care, and preventive approach. Repel price-shoppers without being adversarial.

## Angle / hook
Anti-sales tone. Open with what NOT to look for (red flags) before getting to what to look for. Readers are used to being sold at — this piece treats them as intelligent adults making a considered decision.

## Must-include points
- Difference between NHS and private dental care in practice
- Why 'cheap private' is often worse than good NHS
- What continuity of care actually means and why it matters as you age
- Questions to ask on a first consultation
- Red flags in dental marketing (overpromising aesthetic outcomes, pressure tactics, pricing gimmicks)

## Sources required
- At least one reference to GDC (General Dental Council) guidance
- At least one UK dental health statistic from NHS Digital or equivalent
- Priya's own practice differentiators (pulled from discovery call)

## Length
1,400–1,600 words

## Tone
Match voice profile. Warm but direct. Priya's past pieces emphasised: no jargon, first-person occasional, specific examples from practice life.

## Deadline
Friday of Week 2 (2026-05-08)
